sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), salary DECIMAL(10,2), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, 'Alice', 1000.00, NOW()), (2, 'Bob', NULL, NOW()), (3, '', 2000.50, NULL), (4, 'Charlie', 3000.00, NOW());
SELECT id, quote_dotted('test_data', name) + ' earns ' + CAST(salary AS VARCHAR) AS employee_info FROM test_data WHERE salary IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, quote_dotted('test_data', name) + ' earns ' + CAST(salary AS VARCHAR) AS employee_info FROM test_data WHERE (salary + 0) > 1000;
SELECT id, quote_dotted('test_data', name) AS employee_name FROM test_data WHERE name = '' OR name IS NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY salary) AS salary_count FROM test_data;
SELECT id, quote_dotted('test_data', name) + ' has been recorded ' + CAST(created_at AS VARCHAR) FROM test_data WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data t2 WHERE t2.salary < t1.salary) AS rank FROM test_data t1;
WITH ranked_salaries AS (SELECT id, salary, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY salary) AS salary_rank FROM test_data) SELECT quote_dotted('ranked_salaries', name) FROM ranked_salaries WHERE salary_rank = 1;
SELECT A.id, quote_dotted('test_data', A.name) + ' has salary '+'5000' + ifnull(B.salary, 'Unknown') FROM test_data A LEFT JOIN test_data B ON A.id = B.id + 1;
